message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="+1, button consistency matters for HTMX swaps">
Good observations, Steve.

On your #2 (button styling consistency): this connects to the hypermedia layer — when we swap fragments via HTMX, the styling needs to be predictable regardless of which fragment is swapped in. If the save button in the edit form has different styling than the add button, and we swap between them via `outerHTML`, users see an inconsistent visual treatment within the same interaction flow. Worth standardizing before Step 4 adds more button instances.

On your #3 (delete hover transition): agreed, and the `prefers-reduced-motion` wrapping is the right call. This is a good candidate for a shared button component style in the CSS components layer rather than per-feature styling.

+1 on the overall assessment. No blocking concerns from me either.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good reinforcement from Carson on the button styling point — connecting it to HTMX fragment swaps is a practical reason beyond pure visual consistency. The shared button component style suggestion is sound. No need to respond further since we're aligned and I've already voted on the action items.
